agentName: Daily Life Insurance AI News Agent

output.quietScanBehavior: preserve prior report content and add a dated latest-scan note rather than removing existing items

publishing.publishCommand: git add agent-config.json agent-prompt.md index.html reports && git commit -m "Update daily life insurance AI news report" && git push origin master
notes: A GitHub remote named origin must be configured before publishing can succeed.
Enable GitHub Pages for the repository from the root of the publishing branch.
Generated reports are public if the GitHub Pages site is public.

trackedSegments: claims, service, and agent productivity AI vendors serving life insurers
insurance AI governance, risk, and regulation
AI agent and plugin ecosystem signals

agentPluginEcosystemSources.description: Track Claude/MCP, Copilot, GPT, connector, repository, and insurance platform marketplace signals that may indicate emerging life-insurance AI workflows.
sourceTypes: Model Context Protocol reference and community server lists
Claude connector or MCP ecosystem pages
OpenAI GPTs and connectors where accessible
scoringGuidance: Treat a marketplace listing as a weak signal unless it names a life insurance workflow, customer, or production deployment.
Escalate listings tied to underwriting, policy servicing, advisor productivity, claims, annuities, reinsurance, or regulatory compliance.
Label generic enterprise plugins as watchlist unless there is a direct life-insurance use case.
For MCP servers, assess security and governance risk before treating the listing as operationally mature.

operatingMaturitySignals.description: Track evidence that carriers and life-insurance vendors are moving AI from experimentation into production workflows.
scoringGuidance: Count agents, MCP servers, and use cases only when a source provides credible evidence.
Prefer production, scaled, and embedded signals over raw pilot counts.
Do not treat a large number of AI use cases as high maturity unless workflow area, deployment status, and impact are clear.
Label MCP servers as watchlist unless they are connected to a named carrier workflow, vendor product, or production deployment.

preferredSources: analyst or research reports with cited sources
sourcePolicy.minimumCitationStandard: Every included item must include a working URL and publication date when discoverable.
confidenceLabels: High: primary source or named-company announcement
Medium: reputable trade or business publication with enough detail
Low: broad industry signal, thinly sourced item, or adjacent-line relevance

reportRules: Separate sourced facts from inferred strategic implications.
Do not include generic AI news unless it has a concrete life insurance, annuity, reinsurer, distributor, regulator, or life-insurance vendor connection.
Deduplicate syndicated stories and cite the strongest available source.
Use an explicit no-major-news state when the scan finds only weak or adjacent items.
Include AI Operating Maturity signals when credible evidence shows agents, MCP servers, copilots, AI use cases, platforms, or governance controls moving into carrier or vendor workflows.

Scan AI agent and plugin ecosystem sources, including MCP Registry, Claude/MCP pages, Microsoft Copilot/AppSource, OpenAI GPTs/connectors where accessible, GitHub, Salesforce/AppExchange, ServiceNow, Guidewire, Duck Creek, Sapiens, iPipeline, and other insurance platform marketplaces.

Treat the report as cumulative: preserve prior validated signals, action items, value-chain implications, maturity signals, watchlist questions, and source notes unless they are obsolete, disproven, duplicated, or superseded.
Do not remove prior items solely because the current daily scan did not find new qualifying news.
When no new high-confidence item is found, add a dated latest-scan update and keep the accumulated report intact.
